**Research summary:** External tools (web_search × 2, research_papers snippet_search, local JBC reference) all failed to return usable content. I therefore justify the structure from well-established amyloid-kinetics theory (Knowles/Cohen chemical-kinetics master-equation framework), which is standard and reliable:

- Aβ42 fibril mass follows a sigmoidal (lag → autocatalytic burst → plateau) curve governed by three microscopic processes: **primary nucleation** (sets when the lag ends / onset timing), **elongation** and **secondary nucleation** (autocatalytic, set the steepness once mass exists), and a finite monomer pool (sets the **plateau**).
- Inhibitors partition into those that **delay onset** (suppress primary nucleation → later half-time, longer lag) and those that **slow the autocatalytic burst**
